intro: Ermania microcarpa, also known as Ermania, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae, native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and edible roots. The health benefits of Ermania microcarpa include its potential to support digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ermania microcarpa include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
